a: Databricks is a cloud platform where teams store, clean, and analyze large amounts of data, and build AI on top of it, all in one place. It runs on top of Apache Spark, so the heavy data work gets spread across many machines for you.
fs: Databricks is a unified cloud platform for data and AI, built on Spark, that pulls storage, processing, and analytics into one workspace.
fj: Databricks is just a hosted Jupyter notebook for running Python.

a: A lakehouse is a way of storing data that mixes the best of two older ideas: the cheap, flexible storage of a data lake and the reliable, organized tables of a data warehouse. So you keep all your data in one cheap place but can still run trustworthy queries on it.
fs: A lakehouse adds warehouse-grade tables and reliability on top of cheap data-lake storage, so one copy of data serves both BI and ML.
fj: A lakehouse is just a fancy name for a normal database.

q: What is a cluster in Databricks?
a: A cluster is the group of cloud computers that actually run your code and crunch your data. You pick its size, it spins up when you need it, and it can shut itself off when idle so you stop paying for it.
fs: A cluster is the on-demand compute (driver plus workers) that executes your jobs; autoscaling and auto-termination control cost.
fj: A cluster is where my data is permanently stored.

a: A notebook is an interactive document where you write and run code in small chunks called cells, and see the results right below each one. You can mix Python, SQL, and notes in the same notebook, which makes it easy to explore data step by step.
fs: A notebook is a cell-based interface attached to a cluster, letting you mix Python, SQL, and Markdown and see results inline.
fj: Each cell in a notebook runs in total isolation from the others.

a: Delta Lake is the table format Databricks uses to make your data files safe and reliable. It lets you update or delete rows, and even look at older versions of a table (called time travel), without your files getting corrupted when many jobs run at once.
fs: Delta Lake adds ACID transactions, updates/deletes, and time travel on top of plain files, making the lakehouse tables trustworthy.
fj: Delta Lake is a brand-new database engine that replaces Spark.
